4. data type parsing functions

If we have for example:

We need a pretty smart code, that will be able to parse those patterns.

Basic Datatype Parsing

Section titled “Basic Datatype Parsing”

Datatype Enum

Section titled “Datatype Enum”

We need to implement an enum that will be holding parsed data, let’s name it ‘DataType’. It has to be an enum, because it will be holding different data types, like: arrays, pointers, enums, etc.

But for now, only give it these types:

  • Data-> name and a bool saying whether it is unsigned.
  • Pointer-> Box< DataType >
⚠️ Implementation

Parse Function

Section titled “Parse Function”

Declaration

Section titled “Declaration”

Function

Section titled “Function”
  1. Parse current tokens, so that it outputs appropriate ‘DataType’.
  2. Remember to check, for TokenKind::Unsigned at the beginning. For example: unsigned int uint = 16;
  3. Select the right function depending on the kind of the token it encounters next:
    • identifier -> identifier_type();
    • enum -> enum_type();
    • struct -> struct_type();
  4. Their functions will output Result<DataType>, so we will just output them straight after adding some context.
⚠️ Implementation

Identifier Type

Section titled “Identifier Type”

It has to output Datatype::Data. It will do this by just taking the current token as a name, and a boolean saying weather it is unsigned or not.

Then it will check if our data isn’t wrapped in pointers.Just wrap data in a DataType::Pointer for each token of kind ‘star’.

⚠️ Implementation

Now update call to the ‘identifier_type()’ inside the ‘parse()’. Now you should be able to parse the type from the example. In this tutorial we will be testing this later. After implementing variable declaration, but you might test it out ,right now if you want!

Parsing Arrays

Section titled “Parsing Arrays”

Data Type

Section titled “Data Type”

We need to add an enum type for it to the DataType, it needs to have:

  • length
  • inside ‘DataType’
⚠️ Implementation

Parsing

Section titled “Parsing”

Function to parse an array will not be called by the ‘parse’ function, this is because ‘array brackets’ come after the name of a variable. Because of this, the function will be called by the variable declaration function, so it ‘wraps’ the current datatype inside of the Array data type.

Example

Section titled “Example”

Will be converted into:

Wrap_Data_Type_in_an_Array

Section titled “Wrap_Data_Type_in_an_Array”

Declaration

Section titled “Declaration”

Function

Section titled “Function”

Wraps its input in arrays.It will do this for each [ token. Remember to read the length specified inside of it, and than expect the ] token.

⚠️ Implementation

Parsing Struts

Section titled “Parsing Struts”

Data Type

Section titled “Data Type”

The struct data type just needs to know its properties. We will implement a Property struct inside the expressions.rs, because we will be also using it in other places. It needs to know its name and data type.

⚠️ Implementation

DataType::Struct

⚠️ Implementation

Parsing

Section titled “Parsing”

We need to expect all the curly braces and the struct keyword. Afterwords we need to parse all the properties. To do this:

  1. Get the datatype -> use ‘parse()’
  2. Get variable name -> read value of the next identifier token
  3. Do this until the next character is not a comma.
⚠️ Implementation

Parsing Enums

Section titled “Parsing Enums”

Data Type

Section titled “Data Type”

First we need to define a new type for the enum fields, because it doesn’t match the previous types that we used, it needs: value(int) and a name.

⚠️ Implementation

Type inside the DataType, will only store fields.

⚠️ Implementation

Parsing

Section titled “Parsing”

To parse enum we will have to read the name of a field, then check if it has some value assigned to it. If the value is set, then it should carry through to the next field increased by one.

Caution

Remember to expect tokens like ’{’ and ’}’ at the beginning and end.

⚠️ Implementation

End Result

Section titled “End Result”

At in the end your parser/types/mod.rs file should look like this:

⚠️ Implementation

Bugs

Section titled “Bugs”

If you find anything to improve in this project’s code, please create an issue describing it on the GitHub repository for this project. For website-related issues, create an issue here.

Support

Section titled “Support”
